eras.name: The OG Era
eras.description: Built around the foundational quintet of MortaL, Owais, RonaK, VipeR, and Slayer. Early statistical dominance based on individual mechanical disparity in the pre-ban PUBG Mobile ecosystem.
eras.definingMoment: Back-to-back 1st place at PMIS 2019 and PMCO Spring India, capturing over $100,000.

eras.name: The Transition & Ban Era
eras.description: Strategically dormant period driven by the PUBG Mobile app ban in India. Forced reliance on rotating temporary cast members. Weakest competitive phase with finishes plummeting to 16th-23rd place.
eras.definingMoment: S8UL Esports merger in October 2020, securing long-term financial viability through diversified content creation.

eras.name: The Superteam Era
eras.description: Shifted from legacy influencer-players to dedicated mechanical specialists under strict coaching frameworks. Redefined Team SouL's tactical approach.
eras.definingMoment: BMPS Season 1 victory ($95,935) and PMWI 2022 global representation.

eras.name: The Rebuild & Modern Era
eras.description: Turnkey acquisition of former Blind Esports roster, followed by strategic reintegration of legacy fragger Goblin. Forged the current optimal meta-roster.
eras.definingMoment: Historic BGIS 2026 championship victory — record $105,505 prize and 577,685 peak viewers.

mortal.impact: The foundational pillar of Team SouL. Led the squad to the defining 2019 PMIS and PMCO Spring titles. His massive personal brand formed the bedrock of the S8UL content empire.

owais.impact: Essential to the OG Era's success. Early macro shot-calling secured the $42,828 PMIS 2019 win and $60,000 PMCO Spring win. His departure represented the first major fracture.

ronak.impact: Delivered elite mechanical performance during 2019 domestic sweeps as primary damage dealer. Won 'The Rampage Freak' at PMIS 2019.

viper.impact: Longest-tenured active player from OG era. Provided critical structural stability through the turbulent 2020-2021 roster rotations.

slayer.impact: Part of the original founding five. Departed early in 2019 before the competitive season began.

sc0utop.impact: Both tenures were extraordinarily brief (~2-3 months each). Failed to yield A-Tier victories despite massive fan anticipation, highlighting strategic synergy issues.

clutchgod.impact: Temporary acquisition during the post-exodus rebuild. Helped field a roster for the PMCO Fall Split.

regaltos.impact: Primary aggressive talent during the transitional 2020-2021 phase. Helped secure multiple 2nd place finishes in 2020 invitationals.

aman.impact: Transitioned from player to coaching staff, supporting the Superteam era.

sangwan.impact: Part of the transitional 2020 roster during the PUBG Mobile ban era.

blaezi.impact: Brief two-month stint during the chaotic mid-2020 period.

mavi.impact: Recruited to lead the 2021 BGMI launch roster. Strategic alignment failed with legacy SouL players, leading to departure after three months of bottom-tier finishes.

roxx.impact: Stopgap acquisition to finish the failing 2021 season.

viru.impact: Stopgap acquisition to finish the failing 2021 season.

yuvaop.impact: Stopgap acquisition to finish the failing 2021 season.

deathnote.impact: Stopgap acquisition to finish the failing 2021 season.

omega.impact: Known as 'The Humble IGL.' Architected the team's competitive renaissance. Under his macro-strategy, won BMPS S1 ($95,935) and represented India globally. Organization noted his impact as 'immeasurable.'

akshat.impact: Core member of the Superteam era. Contributed to the BMPS S1 victory and sustained competitiveness through 2023.

hector.impact: Validated international capability by winning the 'Eagle Eye' award ($5,000) at PMWI 2022 and swept multiple utility-focused awards (Saviour, Doctor) during BGMI Masters Series.

goblin.impact: Statistically the most lethal individual player in Team SouL history. His 2022 BMPS S1 performance is historically unprecedented, sweeping MVP, Rampage Freak, Fan Favorite, and three Player of the Day awards. His 2025 return was instrumental in securing the BGIS 2026 championship.

neyo.impact: Joined mid-2023 to bolster the Superteam core. Departed with the rest of the core in December.

manya.impact: Orchestrated the 2024 core that pulled Team SouL out of the post-Omega vacuum. Guided squad to 1st at BGMS S3 ($65,515).

nakul.impact: Current anchor of the organization. Evolved into a premier IGL. His macro-strategies culminated in 'Best IGL' award during the monumental BGIS 2026 championship victory.

jokerr.impact: Two-stint player. Part of the BGMS S3 winning core, departed mid-2024, returned July 2025 to join the BGIS 2026 championship roster.

rony.impact: Part of the January 2024 ex-Blind Esports acquisition. Contributed to BGMS S3 victory.

spower.impact: Part of the January 2024 ex-Blind Esports acquisition. Departed late 2024.

ninjajod.impact: Late-year 2024 adjustment replacing departed players. Played through early 2025.

saumay.impact: Late-year 2024 addition. Brief tenure before early 2025 roster overhaul.

skipz.impact: Late-year 2024 addition. Brief tenure before early 2025 roster overhaul.

legit.impact: Named Finals MVP at BGIS 2026. Raw firepower imported to complement Goblin's fragging and NakuL's IGL framework.

thunder.impact: Latest acquisition, joined September 2025. Part of the BGIS 2026 championship-winning roster.

hunterz.impact: Brief stint in early 2025 before the mid-year roster overhaul.
